message:
docs: Add Q-Healing documentation to README

- Add Q-Healing feature highlights in Key Features section
- Add dedicated Q-Healing section explaining:
  - The problem it solves (30B+ models on 16GB Macs)
  - How it works (layer-by-layer comparison with FP16 reference)
  - Hardware safety gates (battery, thermal, rate limiting)
  - Dream corpus mixing (50/50 reference + user data)
  - State machine diagram (awake → deep_sleep → groggy → awake)
- Update project structure with new source files
- Update storage locations with corpus and healing adapter paths

files.patch:
@@ -18,6 +18,12 @@ Zell is a high-performance local inference engine for running large language mod
 - **Hippocampus architecture** - Unconsolidated memories (recent) vs consolidated (trained)
 - **Zero-config learning** - No manual dataset preparation or training steps
 
+### 🔧 Q-Healing: Run 30B+ Models on 16GB Macs
+- **Quantization Healing** - Train LoRA adapters to correct Q2_K quantization errors
+- **Deep Sleep Mode** - Loads FP16 layers one-at-a-time during idle periods
+- **Hardware Safety Gates** - Blocks on battery, thermal throttling, rate limits to 1x/24h
+- **Dream Corpus Mixing** - 50% reference data + 50% user data prevents overfitting
+
 ### ⚡ Instant Startup
 - **System prompt caching** - Cache KV state during build for instant restoration
 - **Profile-based optimization** - Pre-analyzes models to prevent OOM crashes
@@ -274,7 +280,13 @@ zell lora <model.gguf> <load|list|unload|info>
 │       ├── 19723.jsonl     # Daily logs (days since epoch)
 │       └── 19724.jsonl
 ├── lora/                    # Auto-trained adapters
-│   └── {model_hash}_auto.lora
+│   ├── {model_hash}_auto.lora
+│   └── healing_{model_hash}.lora  # Q-Healing correction adapter
+├── corpus/                  # Reference corpus for Q-Healing
+│   ├── wiki_subset.txt     # General knowledge (~20MB)
+│   ├── code_samples.txt    # Programming patterns (~15MB)
+│   ├── reasoning.txt       # Logic and math (~10MB)
+│   └── manifest.json       # Corpus metadata
 ├── profiles/                # Model optimization profiles
 │   └── {model}.gguf.json
 └── shaders/                 # Pre-compiled Metal shaders
@@ -323,6 +335,100 @@ try logger.markConsolidated(&[_]u64{ 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 });
 
 ---
 
+## Q-Healing: Quantization Healing via Sleep
+
+Q-Healing enables running 30B+ parameter models on 16GB MacBooks by training LoRA adapters that correct quantization errors in aggressively compressed (Q2_K) base models.
+
+### The Problem
+
+When your model is too large for your Mac's memory, you're forced to use aggressive quantization (Q2_K) which significantly damages output quality. Q-Healing recovers this lost quality.
+
+### How It Works
+
+```
+┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+│                    Q-HEALING: DEEP SLEEP CYCLE                       │
+├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+│                                                                      │
+│   1. DETECT IDLE (5+ minutes of no requests)                        │
+│      └─> Check hardware safety gates                                │
+│                                                                      │
+│   2. LOAD HIGH-PRECISION LAYER (one at a time)                      │
+│      ┌─────────────────┐                                            │
+│      │   FP16 Layer 12 │ ← Load from reference model (~400MB)       │
+│      │   (Reference)   │                                            │
+│      └─────────────────┘                                            │
+│                                                                      │
+│   3. COMPARE WITH Q2_K LAYER                                        │
+│      ┌─────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────┐                     │
+│      │   Q2_K Layer 12 │ vs │   FP16 Layer 12 │                     │
+│      │   (Runtime)     │    │   (Reference)   │                     │
+│      └─────────────────┘    └─────────────────┘                     │
+│              │                      │                                │
+│              └──────────┬───────────┘                                │
+│                         │                                            │
+│   4. COMPUTE CORRECTION GRADIENTS                                   │
+│      Error = FP16_output - Q2_K_output                              │
+│      Gradient = d(Error)/d(LoRA_weights)                            │
+│                                                                      │
+│   5. UPDATE HEALING ADAPTER                                         │
+│      ┌─────────────────┐                                            │
+│      │  healing.lora   │ ← Accumulated corrections                  │
+│      │  (~10MB)        │                                            │
+│      └─────────────────┘                                            │
+│                                                                      │
+│   6. FREE LAYER, REPEAT                                             │
+│      └─> Next layer until interrupted or complete                   │
+│                                                                      │
+│   RESULT: Q2_K + healing.lora ≈ Q4/Q8 quality                       │
+│                                                                      │
+└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+```
+
+### Hardware Safety Gates
+
+Q-Healing includes critical safety features for consumer Mac hardware:
+
+| Gate | Condition | Reason |
+|------|-----------|--------|
+| **Battery** | Block on battery power | Prevents power drain |
+| **Thermal** | Block on serious/critical thermal | Prevents throttling |
+| **Rate Limit** | Max 1 cycle per 24 hours | Protects SSD lifespan |
+| **Cooldown** | 1 hour between cycles | Prevents overheating |
+
+### Dream Corpus Mixing
+
+To prevent overfitting during calibration, Q-Healing uses a 50/50 mix:
+
+```
+Calibration Data = 50% Reference Corpus + 50% User Conversations
+                   │                      │
+                   │                      └─> Your actual usage patterns
+                   └─> Wikipedia, code samples, reasoning tasks
+```
+
+**Storage:** `~/.cache/zell/corpus/`
+
+### State Machine
+
+```
+awake ──idle──> pre_drowsy ──> drowsy ──> dreaming ──> deep_sleep
+                                                           │
+                                                      [Q-Healing]
+                                                           │
+                                                           ▼
+                                                        groggy
+                                                           │
+                                                    "Waking up..."
+                                                           │
+                                                           ▼
+                                                        awake
+```
+
+The **groggy** state provides user feedback when deep sleep is interrupted, preventing frozen UI while layer memory is freed.
+
+---
+
 ## Models
 
 Zell supports any GGUF-format model. Due to their large size, models are not included in the repository.

 ```
